preferred_name: Domain - Circuit-breakers and similar equipment for household
definition: domain covering protection devices for household with rated currents not exceeding 125 A and rated voltages not exceeding 440 V
note: The domain covers the following device types:, - circuit-breakers and residual current devices of rated currents not exceeding 125 A and rated voltages not exceeding 440 V for protection against overcurrent and/or against electric shock in domestic and similar installations, , - residual current monitors (RCM) for household and similar applications, , - circuit-breakers for equipment of rated currents not exceeding 125 A and rated voltages not exceeding 440 V designed to protect equipment for use in domestic and similar installations, , - control and protection devices for electric vehicle supplies, , - arc fault detection devices (AFDD) of rated currents not exceeding 125 A and rated voltages not exceeding 440 V for household and similar uses,, - automatic reclosing devices, , - power frequency overvoltage protection devices.

preferred_name: Classes root identifier - Circuit-breakers and similar equipment for household
definition: heading number for classification of circuit-breakers and similar equipment for household use

preferred_name: Classes - Miniature circuit-breakers for overcurrent protection for household and similar installations
definition: circuit-breakers for domestic and similar purpose of rated currents not exceeding 125 A and rated voltages not exceeding 440 V for protection of wiring against overcurrents in domestic and similar installations

mechanical switching device for AC application, capable of making, carrying and breaking currents under normal circuit conditions and also making, carrying for a specified time and breaking currents under specified abnormal circuit conditions such as those of short-circuit
source_document: IEC 60050 441:1984, 441-14-20, modified – “AC miniature” has been added to the term

preferred_name: Blocks root - Circuit-breakers and similar equipment for household
definition: heading number for blocks of properties to describe all types of products within this domain
general technical aspects of the device
all the conductive parts of a switching device included in the circuit which it is designed to close or open
short-circuit conditions, stated by the manufacturer, which the device can make, withstand or break satisfactorily
release which causes a mechanical switching device to open in specified conditions
all the conductive parts of the device which are intended to be included in a circuit other than the main circuit
physical information of the device for installation
terminals, screws or other parts, used for the electrical connection of conductors of external circuits of the device
physical conditions such as ambient temperature, pressure, radiation, humidity, chemical spray expected as normal operating conditions or resulting from postulated initiating events
conformity of a device with specified requirements and compliance with recognized product standards

preferred_name: Specialised blocks root - Circuit-breakers and similar equipment for household
definition: heading number for specialised blocks of properties to describe all types of products within this domain
general technical aspects of the device (specific to AC miniature circuit-breaker)
all the conductive parts of a switching device included in the circuit which it is designed to close or open (specific to AC miniature circuit-breaker)
short-circuit conditions, stated by the manufacturer, which the device can make, withstand or break satisfactorily (specific to AC miniature circuit-breaker)
release which causes a mechanical switching device to open in specified conditions (specific to AC miniature circuit-breaker)
terminals, screws or other parts, used for the electrical connection of conductors of external circuits of the device (specific to AC miniature circuit-breaker)

physical conditions such as ambient temperature, pressure, radiation, humidity, chemical spray expected as normal operating conditions or resulting from postulated initiating events (specific to AC miniature circuit-breaker)
conformity of a device with specified requirements and compliance with recognized product standards (specific to AC miniature circuit-breaker)
information necessary for unambiguous identification of the device

phase to neutral or phase to phase voltage value assigned to the connecting device by the manufacturer, this device being intended to be used in installations, the AC voltage supply of which does not exceed the rated insulation voltage of the connecting device itself
note: Rated insulation voltage is always understood to be between live parts and not between live parts and earth (ground).

definition: power dissipation calculated for one pole on the basis of the voltage drop measured at AC rated current under steady state conditions between its terminals
value of ultimate short-circuit breaking capacity at 230 V AC assigned to that circuit-breaker by the manufacturer
value of ultimate short-circuit breaking capacity at 400 V AC assigned to that circuit-breaker by the manufacturer

preferred_name: energy limiting class according to relevant EN standard
definition: class number (class 1 or class 3) defined by standard EN 60898-1 or EN 61009-1 as relevant

preferred_name: rated making and breaking capacity of an individual pole, AC (Icn1)
definition: value of the AC limiting short-circuit making and breaking capacity on each individual protected pole of multipole circuit breakers
ambient air temperature on which the time-current characteristics are based according to IEC 60898-1, 30°C being the preferred value
minimum value of current causing the circuit-breaker to operate automatically without intentional time-delay

preferred_name: derating of tripping characteristic based on temperature
definition: availability of information on the variation of the tripping characteristic for ambient temperature differing from the reference calibration temperature
specification whether additional equipment providing an additional function is possible
distance between two lateral faces of the device expressed in multiple of 18 mm

preferred_name: mounting depth of the modular device
definition: distance between the surface in contact with the mounting part (e.g. rail) and the location of the protection panel from the switchboard
terminal intended for connection of comb busbar only
one terminal with two clamping units, one for a comb busbar, the other for a conductor
identifier of the type of connection present on the side of the circuit-breaker intended for the connection to the power supply lines
note:
screw terminal: terminal in which the conductor is clamped under the head of the screw and where the clamping pressure can be applied directly by the head of the screw or through an intermediate part, such as a washer, a clamping plate or an anti-spread device
screwless terminal: connecting terminal for the connection and subsequent disconnection of one conductor or the interconnection of two or more conductors capable of being dismantled, the connection being made, directly or indirectly, by means of springs, wedges, eccentrics or cones, etc., without special preparation of the conductor other than removal of insulation
Flat-quick connection termination: electrical connection consisting of a male tab and a female connector which can be pushed into and withdrawn with or without the use of a tool
plug-in terminal: terminal the electrical connection and disconnection of which can be effected without displacing the conductors of the corresponding circuit

definition: identifier of the type of connection present on the side of the circuit-breaker intended for the connection to the load-side lines
note:
screw terminal: terminal in which the conductor is clamped under the head of the screw and where the clamping pressure can be applied directly by the head of the screw or through an intermediate part, such as a washer, a clamping plate or an anti-spread device
screwless terminal: connecting terminal for the connection and subsequent disconnection of one conductor or the interconnection of two or more conductors capable of being dismantled, the connection being made, directly or indirectly, by means of springs, wedges, eccentrics or cones, etc., without special preparation of the conductor other than removal of insulation
Flat-quick connection termination: electrical connection consisting of a male tab and a female connector which can be pushed into and withdrawn with or without the use of a tool
plug-in terminal: terminal the electrical connection and disconnection of which can be effected without displacing the conductors of the corresponding circuit

definition: numeral characterising the expected pollution of the micro-environment

protected mark issued by a body performing third-party conformity assessment, indicating that an object of conformity assessment (product, process, person, system or body) is in conformity with specified requirements
source_document: ISO 17030:2021, 3.1, modified – The term “third-party mark of conformity” is replaced by conformity marks.

definition: numeral defining a transient overvoltage condition
number of electrically separated conducting paths, each provided with a contact intended to connect and disconnect the main circuit of a switching device
circuit-breakers and similar equipment for household use reference standard(s) to which the product complies with

preferred_name: rated operational voltage for IT systems, AC
definition: maximum value of the AC rated operational voltage for which the device is suitable for use on IT systems
limit of the setting range of the AC current causing the operation of a release without any intentional time-delay
reference property linked to the generic block KDF010 for Identification, for assignment to any class
